title: dispatch_peer_deliver: no chat_id existence check before insert_message_ext
description: relay.rs dispatch_peer_deliver passes chat_id directly to insert_message_ext without verifying the chat exists. A peer can broadcast a PeerDeliver with an arbitrary chat_id string; if the chat does not exist, the insert either silently fails (FK constraint) or creates an orphaned message row — the error is only logged at warn level and execution continues. The stored_id returned on error is the peer-supplied message_id (unsanitised). This is distinct from nie-po5v.1 which concerns unvalidated chat_id in dispatch_peer_deliver in context of the daemon dispatcher; confirming it is the same path.
